comment.body: Trades COME AND GO. Please focus on Education. I REALY just want to be able to re-create the charts and tools or something close to what she has been able to create. Then analyze and INTERPRET them is KEY. Then some tips on timing a trade or approach to execution if possible. I did like the swing trade idea for this gives people a better chance to manage. Another idea would be a "How to Course". Some very knowledgeable people actually do plan-forecast trades with weeks/months in advance, then just constantly monitor if environment and conditions are setting up as forecasted. As time passes that forecast either materializes or not (like the weather). And then averages into the trade. If does not work then move on to already forecasted next trade. But if works then says "I got lucky". I love that.

comment.body: Alma: May I propose something straightforward? Only swing trades on SP500. Associated with the green and the red dots.  We don't need to have all your trades. Personally, knowing that you are in a swing trade in one direction will give me more confidence in my biais. I have my trades, my SL and I manage them and I think most of us do the same. If you don't have a swing trade, you could just post (no swing trade). This is just an idea, open to criticism.

comment.body:
Ya definitely fair. And I understand that trading signals isn’t your intent. But, letting us see how you implement the data is helpful. Also, for those of you who say “it’s up to you to use the data and make your own decisions”, fair point, but it’s still vitally important to have a “proof of concept”. You definitely executed that yesterday Alma and congrats on the excellent call.

You are juggling a lot, and being a fellow creative thinker, I can understand how things slip through the cracks and how you might not be running things like a Toyota factory. But there is a balance, and you understand where I’m coming from there. Hence why I try to give feedback to help with the caveat that it’s always easier said than done.

In terms of calling your change of outlook in March OPEX “dramatic”, this emphasizes the importance of choosing words carefully. I believe we had a very bullish sentiment going into March expo and post NVDA earnings, as evidenced by when you entered those calls last week.

Now, we got those weaselly pros sending us on the stairway to hell while they eat thirsty bears alive like a Komodo Dragon, with high put premiums that will be hard to realize profit on, along with fomo bulls who will see their intermediate/longer term term calls expire at zero. Nobody will be making money on a swing/intermediate basis except those that are shorting straight shares. Or short term degens like us who will snipe in and out, in favor of bounces on the call side.

So I don’t think “dramatic” is too dramatic 😜. But maybe so lol. Either way, it’s different.

Thanks for responding, taking my feedback with consideration, and being a kind listener.

comment.body:
this is why i think i will close that thread, or at least clear it fully, and only give intraday signals when I trade, and on significant inflection points, bcs i just not have the capacity to post everything i do, or my algos do. 
it would be way easier to make via api, but tbh i never planned to give trading signals as written on twitter. and i know that in this format this is just a mess. 
ADI was closed in profit, the others were closed or stopped out on friday this is why i was done with open positions. 
if i remember correctly, i mentioned this in the daily chat maybe, but i only said about the call was closed with loss.
to not take longer-term positions was said in the posts, signaling that i don't see edge on holding longer-term positions. however note that sometimes traders chose 3-4dtes only bcs of a better expected payoff. 
plp asked me then when i still took those longs on nvda, aapl etc. that why i did so depsite what i wrote, and i said it is for short-term trade... just like an intraday trade. 
So yeah, i understand where the confusion lies, totally. and this was the original reason why i didn't want to give trading signals at all only analysis. 
...
if you don't get answer from me, tag me then. i have a lot of things to do beside this, i'm not full-time subscripe content provider, its like 30% of my time, including coding, thinking and writing the posts + deal with the stripe issues daily, letters and private messages too. so i can miss some messages.
i need to re-organize things in my life, bcs i don't like it this way either. the issue with daily post is always about the data providing, bcs i want the most fresh data i can take. 
...
my outlook didn't changed dramatically, you just didn't followed me ;))

comment.body:
Yo, pay attention for a sec..especially those with less experience.
Sometimes Alma doesn’t give exact timing, and that’s because sometimes you just can’t. And there’s nothing worse than someone saying “Wednesday”, only for the move to actually happen on Tuesday.
What Alma does is exactly what every good quant in the industry should be doing..she lays out the environment, explains cause and effect, and shows where transitions happen.
This picture gets sharper over time, but you already know what to look for.
So the scenario is mapped out, along with its invalidations (though yeah, Alma tends to be pretty straightforward with her bias), and this scenario keeps getting clearer as time progresses.
There are very few moments when you can say with pinpoint accuracy what’s going to happen and when.
Last week’s Thursday reversion call was one of those moments..I could tell Alma was 100% sure. The logic was airtight.
But it’s not always that clean, especially when there’s a ton of 0DTE activity

comment.body: to manage trades I use SETUP system: S-Stop E-Entry T-Target U-Uphold your P-Plan. Trading is not a sure thing. It is a bet. Only difference is that it is educated bet if following that plan (statistical edge). Unfortunately humans are not wired for that. But luckily humans with discipline could mimic a machine or algo LOL.

comment.body:
Nah, this is just nitpicking now.
First off, plenty of people got it and traded off Alma’s signals, you can literally see it.
Second, it’s not our business how alma trades. she gives clear analysis, and everyone trades it their own way. Different accounts, different risk profiles, different objectives.
And bro, you weren’t even paying attention. Alma only held an ES position long-term the past weeks. Everything else was intraday or1-2 days at max! She straight-up said risk was high, and she explained exactly that this means big spot fluctuation.
Nobody was extremely bullish. All she said was bullish edge, call side edge, market won't crash, etc.. Then thursday came, and she flagged it in time. She also kept saying that things clear up post-feb opex.

comment.body:
When everyone was bearish, she said BTD.
When everyone was screaming moon, she said reversion, long puts.
And now, when everyone called for doomsday, she bought calls and broke it down for us perfectly.
Does this stuff just fly over your head, or what?
And what juggling, what proof of concept, man.
Lemme tell you something. In the industry, there are insanely good quant analysts who are terrible traders. But they predict what needs to be predicted, they calculate positioning, they lay out the structures. they just can’t execute trades for themselves.
What we take from the analysis is on us.
What if i trade pairs, or follow discretionary strategies? Alma’s analysis still helps.
We shouldn’t even go down this road, man.
The trading signal chat isn’t part of the service. She made that crystal clear on X.
Being a good trader yourself doesn’t mean she has to be one for you.

comment.body:
Ya definitely fair. And I understand that trading signals isn’t your intent. But, letting us see how you implement the data is helpful. Also, for those of you who say “it’s up to you to use the data and make your own decisions”, fair point, but it’s still vitally important to have a “proof of concept”. You definitely executed that yesterday Alma and congrats on the excellent call.

You are juggling a lot, and being a fellow creative thinker, I can understand how things slip through the cracks and how you might not be running things like a Toyota factory. But there is a balance, and you understand where I’m coming from there. Hence why I try to give feedback to help with the caveat that it’s always easier said than done.

In terms of calling your change of outlook in March OPEX “dramatic”, this emphasizes the importance of choosing words carefully. I believe we had a very bullish sentiment going into March expo and post NVDA earnings, as evidenced by when you entered those calls last week.

Now, we got those weaselly pros sending us on the stairway to hell while they eat thirsty bears alive like a Komodo Dragon, with high put premiums that will be hard to realize profit on, along with fomo bulls who will see their intermediate/longer term term calls expire at zero. Nobody will be making money on a swing/intermediate basis except those that are shorting straight shares. Or short term degens like us who will snipe in and out, in favor of bounces on the call side.

So I don’t think “dramatic” is too dramatic 😜. But maybe so lol. Either way, it’s different.

Thanks for responding, taking my feedback with consideration, and being a kind listener.
